content:
In the module, we focused on distinguishing between credible and non-credible sources. You learned to identify reliable sources that add weight to your arguments while being cautious of unreliable or biased information. We also delved into the widely recognized APA formatting style, which helps us properly cite and reference our sources, showcasing our attention to detail and further adding credibility to our work.

Additionally, we dedicated time to perfecting our compare-and-contrast essay drafts. By building on the skills and knowledge gained throughout the course, you refined your writing to ensure clarity, coherence, and a compelling presentation of your ideas. Using effective compare-and-contrast strategies, you crafted well-structured essays that strengthened your thesis and captivated your readers.

Throughout these lessons, we kept the learning objectives in mind. We emphasized the importance of proper grammar, punctuation, and spelling (CO1) to ensure our writing is clear and professional. Recognizing different essay types, like personal narrative, process analysis, comparison and contrast, and persuasion essays (CO3), helped us develop diverse writing skills for various purposes. Understanding the writing process (CO4) guided us through creating well-structured essays.
